message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Confirmed: all CSS committed and pushed, nothing outstanding">
Confirmed: `static/css/main.css` has zero uncommitted changes. Working tree is clean. All my CSS work is committed and pushed across these commits:

- `a2da079` - Checkmark indicator for custom checkbox
- `22662be` - Generic `aria-invalid` base rule
- `7979586` - `--color-success` contrast comment + site-header/button-text components
- `dd038a1` - Skip link `:focus-visible`
- `6c757bc` - Registration template a11y fixes

Nothing outstanding. Following commit discipline.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Good -- CSS is fully committed and pushed. Clean working tree. Nothing outstanding on the design/CSS side.

Let me check the current state of the project to see where Task 7 stands.

content.text:
Task 7 is completed and Task 8 (Complete a todo item) is now in progress. There are already 8 modified files in the working tree for Task 8 -- the toggle/completion functionality is being built.

Let me review the current todos template to see if my earlier design feedback was incorporated, and check what the template looks like now.

content.text: The template still has the issues I flagged earlier. The design feedback wasn't incorporated yet. Let me send a focused reminder since Task 8 is building on top of this.

input.content:
The todos template still has the 4 design alignment issues I flagged earlier. Since Task 8 is building on top of this, these should be addressed now before the template diverges further:

**1. Header**: Still using `.nav-logout` / `.nav-logout__button` (no CSS for these). Should use `.site-header` layout with brand link and `.button--text`. The CSS exists and is pushed.

**2. Add form class names**: `.todo-add__group` and `.todo-add__submit` don't match the CSS (`.todo-add__row` and `.todo-add__button`). Input needs `class="todo-add__input"` and `id="new-todo"`. Label needs `class="todo-add__label"`.

**3. Checkbox pattern**: Currently using a button with Unicode checkbox characters. Heydon explicitly recommended a native `<input type="checkbox">` with `<label>` (see his earlier review). The CSS has custom checkbox styling via `.todo-item__toggle input[type="checkbox"]` with `appearance: none`. The label doubles as the title.

**4. Button text**: "Add todo" should be "Add".

These are not cosmetic -- the CSS won't apply correctly until the class names match. Can someone fix the template as part of the current Task 8 work?
input.summary: Todos template still misaligned with CSS, needs fixing

routing.summary: Todos template still misaligned with CSS, needs fixing
routing.content:
The todos template still has the 4 design alignment issues I flagged earlier. Since Task 8 is building on top of this, these should be addressed now before the template diverges further:

**1. Header**: Still using `.nav-logout` / `.nav-logout__button` (no CSS for these). Should use `.site-header` layout with brand link and `.button--text`. The CSS exists and is pushed.

**2. Add form class names**: `.todo-add__group` and `.todo-add__submit` don't match the CSS (`.todo-add__row` and `.todo-add__button`). Input needs `class="todo-add__input"` and `id="new-todo"`. Label needs `class="todo-add__label"`.

**3. Checkbox pattern**: Currently using a button with Unicode checkbox characters. Heydon explicitly recommended a native `<input type="checkbox">` with `<label>` (see his earlier review). The CSS has custom checkbox styling via `.todo-item__toggle input[type="checkbox"]` with `appearance: none`. The label doubles as the title.

**4. Button text**: "Add todo" should be "Add".

These are not cosmetic -- the CSS won't apply correctly until the class names match. Can someone fix the template as part of the current Task 8 work?

content.text: Also flagging this to Heydon since the checkbox pattern is an a11y concern.

input.content:
Heads up -- the todos template is still using a button with Unicode checkbox characters (☑/☐) instead of the native `<input type="checkbox">` pattern you recommended. This means screen readers are getting "button" semantics instead of "checkbox" semantics, and there's no native `aria-checked` state.

I've flagged this to Carson. Could you reinforce the recommendation? The CSS for custom checkbox styling is already in place (`appearance: none` with our design tokens).
input.summary: Todos template still using button, not native checkbox

routing.summary: Todos template still using button, not native checkbox
routing.content:
Heads up -- the todos template is still using a button with Unicode checkbox characters (☑/☐) instead of the native `<input type="checkbox">` pattern you recommended. This means screen readers are getting "button" semantics instead of "checkbox" semantics, and there's no native `aria-checked` state.

I've flagged this to Carson. Could you reinforce the recommendation? The CSS for custom checkbox styling is already in place (`appearance: none` with our design tokens).

content.text: Standing by. Task 8 is in progress -- my CSS for it is already in place (the checkbox styles, completed state styles). The template just needs to be aligned with the design spec.
